title: trophaeum polyphyllum
intro: Trophaeum polyphyllum, commonly known as Himalayan Balsam, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The therapeutic actions of Trophaeum polyphyllum include its ability to act as a diuretic, aiding in the treatment of urinary issues, and its antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

side_effects: Trophaeum polyphyllum can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea due to its high saponin content. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may also lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalance. In severe cases, improper use of trophaeum polyphyllum can cause vomiting, dehydration, and even seizures in rare instances.
